title: Qualys VMDR - Detection And KnowledgeBase Metrics Extraction
applies_to: Qualys VMDR vulnerability metrics by asset, asset scope, and tag
Snapshot and period-based reporting built from classic VM APIs

confirmed_facts.fact: The Host Detection List API returns the latest vulnerability data and complete host vulnerability statuses NEW, ACTIVE, FIXED, and REOPENED, plus history information.

Detection records include fields such as UNIQUE_VULN_ID, QID, TYPE, SEVERITY, RESULTS, STATUS, FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME, LAST_FOUND_DATETIME, TIMES_FOUND, LAST_UPDATE_DATETIME, and LAST_PROCESSED_DATETIME in the sample XML output.

Host wrapper fields in host detection output include IP, tracking information, DNS data, NETBIOS, LAST_SCAN_DATETIME, and LAST_VM_SCANNED_DATE; the API can also show ASSET_ID when show_asset_id=1 is requested.
The KnowledgeBase API route used by integrations is /api/2.0/fo/knowledge_base/vuln/, and its details parameter supports Basic, All, or None, where All returns full vulnerability details.
The KnowledgeBase API supports filters such as ids, id_min, id_max, is_patchable, last_modified_after/before, and published_after/before.
KnowledgeBase vulnerability output includes fields such as QID, VULN_TYPE, SEVERITY_LEVEL, TITLE, and other metadata fields referenced by the XML examples and parameter descriptions.

The Vulnerability Details UI page documents QID, Title, QDS, and Severity as first-class columns for the vulnerability view, reinforcing that these are the primary dimensions for dashboard-like vulnerability reporting.
Qualys severity levels are KB-centric service-assigned values that can also be customer-edited by managers in the KnowledgeBase.

recommended_incremental_strategy: Use detection_updated_since/before for change windows when the report is about status transitions or latest changed findings.
Use vm_processed_after/before when the report window should align to host vulnerability processing time.
Refresh KB details separately and only for QIDs present in the scoped detection set, unless a full KB sync is explicitly required.

implementation_notes: Normalize one row per detection instance per asset. UNIQUE_VULN_ID should be retained when present so repeated detections of the same QID on the same host can still be reasoned about safely.
Join detection rows to KB rows by QID to obtain title, KB severity, CVEs, vendor references, and other descriptive metadata required for executive reporting.
If the user asks for reports by vulnerability name, use KB TITLE as the canonical name dimension rather than detection RESULTS text.

For current-state reports, filter to statuses that remain open in the current extraction. For activity reports, use the relevant detection timestamps and status transitions inside the requested period.
Treat SEVERITY from the detection feed and SEVERITY_LEVEL from KnowledgeBase as related but not identical dimensions; preserve both.
caveats: Qualys does not document a dedicated VMDR dashboard metrics endpoint in the reviewed sources, so the rollups must be computed externally.

The reviewed docs show the fields needed for reporting, but they do not define one universal rule for whether a user means first-found, last-found, fixed-date, or last-updated when asking for a period summary. The report builder must make that timestamp choice explicit.
KB metadata is not a substitute for current detection state. A QID can exist in the KnowledgeBase even when it is not currently detected in the requested asset scope.
